refinement_sections.content: The primary goal is to develop a syntactic parser capable of analyzing English sentences, focusing on identifying and labeling syntactic structures. The parser must accurately handle declarative sentences, questions, and commands, ensuring comprehensive coverage of these sentence types.

The parser will prioritize declarative sentences but must also effectively parse interrogative and imperative forms. The design will take into account the limitations of computational resources, aiming for a balance between accuracy and efficiency. We will not cover non-English languages or dialects in this project.

The parser will incorporate contemporary linguistic theories such as Minimalism and Head-Driven Phrase Structure Grammar (HPSG) to inform its syntactic analysis. These theories will guide the development of parsing algorithms and the structure of the output.

Since no specific sentence dataset is provided, we will curate a dataset comprising a diverse range of sentence structures, including various types of declarative sentences, questions, and commands. The dataset will be sourced from linguistic corpora and validated for syntactic complexity and representativeness.

Performance evaluation will be based on precision, recall, and F1-score metrics, comparing the parser's output against a gold standard annotated dataset. We will also consider computational efficiency, measuring parsing speed and resource utilization to align with industry standards.

A comprehensive report will accompany the software prototype, detailing design choices, assumptions made during development, and the rationale for selected linguistic theories and evaluation metrics. This report will also include user guidelines for operating the parser and interpreting its output.
